key_insight: The paper introduces a lowest-order mixed finite element method for strain gradient elasticity by treating the physically meaningful third-order double stress tensor as a primary variable, enabling stable and robust discretization without high-order bubble enrichment or Nitsche boundary treatment.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical, parameter-robust solver for SGE in arbitrary dimensions, with reliable performance across difficult regimes involving small length scales and large Lamé parameters.
method: Uses a third-order tensor variable \(\Phi=\iota^2\,\mathrm{grad}\,\sigma(u)\) in a distributional mixed formulation.
Approximates double stress with an \(\mathbb{S}\otimes\mathbb{R}^d\)-valued lowest-order Raviart--Thomas extension and displacement with vector-valued linear Crouzeix--Raviart elements.
Adds local quadratic post-processing and a hybridized formulation for improved practicality.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports parameter-robust discrete stability, optimal first-order error for fixed parameters, and a parameter-uniform \(\mathcal{O}(\iota^{1/2}+h)\) estimate, supported by numerical experiments in two and three dimensions.
watch_item: Implementation may be nontrivial because the method relies on a specialized tensor-valued mixed element and hybridization/post-processing machinery.
paper_title: A Lowest-Order Robust Mixed Finite Element Method with a Third-Order Tensor Variable for Strain Gradient Elasticity
